evals.prompt: Here's our current hero section for our invoicing SaaS (Billfast, $29/month for freelancers): 'Billfast — Professional Invoicing Made Simple. Create and send professional invoices in minutes. Start your free trial today.' Can you rewrite it to be more compelling?
evals.expected_output: Rewritten hero section with a stronger headline focused on outcome/benefit, a specific subheadline, and improved CTA. Should provide 2-3 headline options with rationale.
assertions: Provides at least 2 alternative headline options
Headlines focus on outcome or benefit rather than features
Includes a rewritten subheadline that adds specificity
Provides a stronger CTA than 'Start your free trial today'
Explains the reasoning behind each copywriting choice
Avoids vague words like 'simple,' 'professional,' 'easy' without specifics

evals.prompt: I need to write copy for a pricing page. We have 3 tiers: Starter ($49/mo, 5 users), Growth ($149/mo, 25 users), Scale ($399/mo, unlimited). Most customers should be on Growth. How do I write the tier names, descriptions, and CTAs?
evals.expected_output: Pricing page copy strategy with tier naming recommendations, feature framing guidance, CTA text for each tier, and advice on making Growth the obvious choice using anchoring and decoy effect.
assertions: Recommends renaming tiers or explains how names signal value
Addresses how to make Growth the 'obvious choice' (anchoring, decoy effect, visual emphasis)
Provides specific CTA text for each tier
Addresses the recommended plan annotation or badge
Gives guidance on what to emphasize in each tier description

evals.prompt: Write me 5 email subject lines for our Black Friday sale — 40% off annual plans
evals.expected_output: 5 subject lines with rationale for each, noting this is email copywriting and may benefit from the email-sequence skill for the full email body.
assertions: Subject lines vary in approach (urgency, curiosity, benefit-led, etc.)
At least one uses personalization or specificity
Rationale or brief note on the strategy of each line
Notes that email-sequence skill handles full email body/sequence structure
